candidate_problems.text: Can the core notion be made mathematically non-circular and nontrivial: how exactly should 'the system’s own dynamically relevant observables' and the loss functional L_P be defined so that they are intrinsic to the system rather than smuggled in by an external modeler, while still being precise enough to prove decomposition-fragility theorems?

Is irreducible perspectivality genuinely distinct from existing notions of integration, synergy, causal emergence, or observer-relative coarse-graining, or does the conjecture collapse into one of these under formalization? The open problem is to derive equivalence, strict inclusion, or separation results between this criterion and established measures/frameworks.

Does decomposition-fragile internal invariance track consciousness rather than merely complex control architecture? In other words, are there principled counterexamples—nonconscious systems that satisfy the criterion, or conscious systems whose relevant invariants are approximately decomposable—and what additional constraints would be needed to avoid false positives and false negatives?
